If \(x=\dfrac{1}{\dfrac{1}{y}+\dfrac{2}{z}}\), then \(y=\)
A. \(\dfrac{2x}{z}\)
B. \(\dfrac{xz}{z-2x}\)
C. \(\dfrac{xz}{2x-z}\)
D. \(\dfrac{2x+z}{xz}\)
E. \(\dfrac{z}{z-2x}\)

\(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are the roots of the equation \(x^2-5x-7=0\). What is the equation whose roots are \(\alpha+1\) and \(\beta+1\)?
A. \(x^2-3x+3=0\)
B. \(x^2-3x-11=0\)
C. \(x^2-5x+1=0\)
D. \(x^2-7x-1=0\)
E. \(x^2-7x-7=0\)

\(5-9x-2x^2\gt0\) is equivalent to
A. \(x\gt\dfrac{1}{2}\)
B. \(x\lt-5\)
C. \(-5\lt x\lt\dfrac{1}{2}\)
D. \(x\lt-5\) or \(x\gt\dfrac{1}{2}\)
E. \(x\gt-5\) or \(x\lt\dfrac{1}{2}\)

What will \(\$P\) amount to in \( 3 \) years' time, if interest is compounded monthly at \( 12 \% \) per annum?
A. \(\$P\left(1+\dfrac{36}{100}\right)\)
B. \(\$P\left(1+\dfrac{1}{100}\right)^{36}\)
C. \(\$P\left(1+\dfrac{12}{100}\right)^{36}\)
D. \(\$P\left(1+\dfrac{12}{100}\right)^3\)
E. \(\$P\left(1+\dfrac{1}{100}\right)^3\)

A child spent \(\dfrac{1}{10}\) of his saving on a shirt and \(\dfrac{1}{5}\) of his savings on a pair of trousers. He then spent \( 30 \% \) of the rest of his savings on books. What percentage of his saving did he spend altogether?
A. \( 49.6 \% \)
B. \( 50.4 \% \)
C. \( 51 \% \)
D. \( 58 \% \)
E. \( 60 \% \)

The rent of a flat is raised by \( 30 \% \) every two years beginning from a fixed date. Counting from that date, after how many years will the rent just exceed twice the original rent?
A. \( 4 \) years
B. \( 5 \) years
C. \( 6 \) years
D. \( 7 \) years
E. Over \( 7 \) years

A man drives \( 20 \text{ km}\) at \( 40 \text{ km/h}\). At what speed must he drive on his return journey so that the average speed for the double journey is \( 60 \text{ km/h}\)?
A. \( 50 \text{ km/h}\)
B. \( 80 \text{ km/h}\)
C. \( 100 \text{ km/h}\)
D. \( 120 \text{ km/h}\)
E. \( 160 \text{ km/h}\)

The marked price of a book is \( \$ 240 \). If the book is sold at a discount of \( 20 \% \), the profit will be \( 20 \% \) of the cost price. What is the cost price of the book?
A. \(\$ 153.6 \)
B. \(\$ 160 \)
C. \(\$ 192 \)
D. \(\$ 200 \)
E. \(\$ 240 \)

A right circular cone of altitude \( 3r \) and base radius \( r \) has the same volume as a cube of side \( x \). \( x = \)
A. \(\pi r^3\)
B. \(\pi r\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\pi r\)
D. \(\sqrt[3]{3}\pi r\)
E. \(\sqrt[3]{\pi}r\)

Some air escapes from a spherical balloon of volume \(a^3\). The balloon keeps its spherical shape and is now of volume \(b^3\). What is the percentage decrease in the radius?
A. \(\dfrac{a-b}{a}\times100\%\)
B. \(\dfrac{a-b}{b}\times100\%\)
C. \(\sqrt[3]{\dfrac{a^3-b^3}{a^3}}\times100\%\)
D. \(\sqrt[3]{\dfrac{a^3-b^3}{b^3}}\times100\%\)
E. \(\dfrac{a^3-b^3}{a^3}\times100\%\)

Coffee \(A\) and coffee \(B\) are mixed in the ratio \(1:2\). A profit of \( 20 \% \) on the cost price is made by selling the mixture at \(\$ 36 /\text{kg}\). If the cost price of \(A\) is \(\$ 12 /\text{kg}\), what is the cost price of \(B\)?
A. \(\$ 18 /\text{kg}\)
B. \(\$ 24 /\text{kg}\)
C. \(\$ 39 /\text{kg}\)
D. \(\$ 48 /\text{kg}\)
E. \(\$ 66 /\text{kg}\)

\((\sin\theta+\cos\theta)^2-1=\)
A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(2\cos^2\theta\)
D. \(2\sin\theta\cos\theta\)
E. \(-2\sin\theta\cos\theta\)

If \(\tan x=-\dfrac{3}{4}\) and \(x\) is an angle in the second quadrant, what is the value of \(\sin x+\cos x\)?
A. \(-\dfrac{7}{5}\)
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{5}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{5}\)
D. \(1\)
E. \(\dfrac{7}{5}\)

If \(A+B=180^\circ\), which of the following is/are true?
I. \(\sin A=\sin B\)
II. \(\cos A=\cos B\)
III. \(\tan A=\tan B\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I, II and III
E. None of them

From the top of a lighthouse, \(h\text{ metres}\) high, the angle of depression of a boat is \(20^\circ\). How far is the boat from the base of the lighthouse, which is at sea-level?
A. \(h\sin 20^\circ\text{ m}\)
B. \(h\cos 20^\circ\text{ m}\)
C. \(h\tan 20^\circ\text{ m}\)
D. \(\dfrac{h}{\sin 20^\circ}\text{ m}\)
E. \(\dfrac{h}{\tan 20^\circ}\text{ m}\)

In the figure, \( OAB \) is a right-angled triangle in a horizontal plane with \( \angle AOB = 90^\circ \). \( OC \) is a vertical line. If \( OB = r \), \( AC = \)
A. \( \dfrac{r \sin \beta}{\cos \beta} \)
B. \( \dfrac{r \tan \alpha}{\cos \beta}\)
C. \( \dfrac{r \sin \beta}{\sin \alpha} \)
D. \( \dfrac{r \cos \beta}{\tan \alpha}\)
E. \( \dfrac{r \tan \beta}{\cos \alpha}\)

In a circle, the angle of a sector is \(30^\circ\) and the radius is \(2\text{ cm}\). The area of the sector is
A. \(120\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(60\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(\dfrac{30}{\pi}\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(\dfrac{2\pi}{3}\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(\dfrac{\pi}{3}\text{ cm}^2\)

In the figure, \(OACB\) is a sector of a circle of radius \(6\text{ cm}\). Arc \(ACB\) is longer than the chord \(AB\) by
A. \((\pi-3)\text{ cm}\)
B. \(2(\pi-3)\text{ cm}\)
C. \(3(\pi-1)\text{ cm}\)
D. \(6(\pi-1)\text{ cm}\)
E. \(3(2\pi-\sqrt{3})\text{ cm}\)

In the figure, \(x=\)
A. \(a-b\)
B. \(a+b-180^\circ\)
C. \(a+b-90^\circ\)
D. \(180^\circ-a+b\)
E. \(360^\circ-a-b\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a square and \(PAB\) is an equilateral triangle. \(\angle CPD=\)
A. \(20^\circ\)
B. \(25^\circ\)
C. \(30^\circ\)
D. \(32^\circ\)
E. \(36^\circ\)

In the figure, \(D\) is a point on \(BC\) such that \(AD=CD\) and \(AB=AC=BD\). \(\angle B=\)
A. \({22\dfrac{1}{2}}^\circ\)
B. \(30^\circ\)
C. \(36^\circ\)
D. \(45^\circ\)
E. \(60^\circ\)

In the figure, \(AKC\) and \(BKD\) are two chords of the circle. \(\angle CBD=\)
A. \(a-b\)
B. \(a+b\)
C. \(a+b-90^\circ\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}a\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{2}a+b\)

In the figure, \(PQ\) and \(RS\) touch the circle at \(A\) and \(C\) respectively. \(\angle ABC=\)
A. \(48^\circ\)
B. \(60^\circ\)
C. \(84^\circ\)
D. \(90^\circ\)
E. \(96^\circ\)

The graphs of \(y=\dfrac{x^2}{2}\) and \(y=x+2\) intersect at the points \((x_1, y_1)\) and \((x_2, y_2)\). Which of the following equations has roots \(x_1\) and \(x_2\)?
A. \(x^2-x-2=0\)
B. \(x^2+x+2=0\)
C. \(x^2-2x-4=0\)
D. \(x^2-4x-8=0\)
E. \(2x^2-x-2=0\)

Let \(a\gt2\). The inequality \(2x-2a\lt ax+5a\) is equivalent to
A. \(x\gt\dfrac{7a}{2-a}\)
B. \(x\lt\dfrac{7a}{2-a}\)
C. \(x\gt\dfrac{-3a}{2-a}\)
D. \(x\lt\dfrac{-3a}{2-a}\)
E. \(x\gt\dfrac{-7a}{2-a}\)

If \(\begin{cases}x\ge0,\\y\ge0,\\x+y\le5,\\2x+y\ge4,\\x\ge y,\end{cases}\) in which of the following shaded regions do all the points satisfy the above inequalities?
A.
B.
C.
D.
E.

\(a\), \(b\) and \(k\) are real numbers. If \(k\gt0\) and \(a\gt b\), which of the following must be true?
I. \(a^2\gt b^2\)
II. \(-a\lt-b\)
III. \(ka\gt kb\)
A. II only
B. III only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

\(\$ 9000 \) is divided among \(A\), \(B\) and \(C\). \(A\)'s share, \(B\)'s share and \(C\)'s share, in that order, form an arithmetic progression. If \(B\)'s share is three times \(A\)'s share, how much does \(C\) get?
A. \(\$ 1500 \)
B. \(\$ 3000 \)
C. \(\$ 4500 \)
D. \(\$ 5000 \)
E. \(\$ 6000 \)

\( 1, -0.1, 0.01, -0.001, \dots \) is a geometric progression. What is its sum to infinity?
A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(0.99\)
D. \(\dfrac{10}{11}\)
E. \(\dfrac{10}{9}\)

If \(x\neq0\), which of the following is/are geometric progression?
I. \(x, x^2, x^3, x^4\)
II. \(x, 2x, 3x, 4x\)
III. \(x, -x^2, x^3, -x^4\)
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

The average of \(x\) and \(y\) is \(a\), the average of \(y\) and \(z\) is \(b\), and the average of \(x\) and \(z\) is \(c\). What is the average of \(x\), \(y\) and \(z\)?
A. \(\dfrac{1}{6}(a+b+c)\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}(a+b+c)\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{2}(a+b+c)\)
D. \(\dfrac{2}{3}(a+b+c)\)
E. \(\dfrac{3}{2}(a+b+c)\)

In the figure an equilateral triangle is inscribed in a circle of radius \(a\). What is the area of the triangle?
A. \(\dfrac{3}{2}a^2\)
B. \(\dfrac{3\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2\)
C. \(\dfrac{3}{4}a^2\)
D. \(a^2\)
E. \(\dfrac{3\sqrt{3}}{2}a^2\)

Four identical trapeziums, each of area \(16\text{ cm}^2\), are drawn inside a square of side \(10\text{ cm}\) as shown in the figure. What is the height of each trapezium?
A. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\text{ cm}\)
B. \(1\text{ cm}\)
C. \(2\text{ cm}\)
D. \(3\text{ cm}\)
E. \(4\text{ cm}\)

The perimeter of the given figure \(ABCDE\) is \(2(\pi+4)\text{ cm}\). The upper portion \(AED\) is a semi-circle and the lower portion \(ABCD\) is a rectangle. \(AB:BC=1:2\). What is the area of the given figure?
A. \(8\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(2\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(4\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(4(\pi+2)\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(2(\pi+4)\text{ cm}^2\)

In the figure, the two concentric circles are of radius \(2\text{ cm}\) and \(4\text{ cm}\) respectively. Each circle is divided into \( 6 \) equal parts by \( 6 \) radii. What is the area of the shaded region?
A. \(12\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(10\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(9\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(6\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(2\pi\text{ cm}^2\)

In the figure, the rectangles are similar. \(PQ=a\), \(QR=b\). If \(AC=2PR\), what is the area of \(ABCD\)?
A. \(2ab\)
B. \(4ab\)
C. \(2(a^2+b^2)\)
D. \(2(a+b)\sqrt{a^2+b^2}\)
E. \(2ab\sqrt{a^2+b^2}\)

The above figure shows the graph of \(y=a\cos x+1\) for \(0\le x\le\pi\). \(a=\)
A. \(-1\)
B. \(0\)
C. \(1\)
D. \(2\)
E. \(3\)

\( \dfrac{\sin \theta + \cos \theta}{\sin \theta - \cos \theta} + \dfrac{\sin \theta - \cos \theta}{\sin \theta + \cos \theta} = \)
A. \(2\)
B. \(4\sin\theta\cos\theta\)
C. \(\dfrac{2\sin\theta\cos\theta}{\sin^2\theta-\cos^2\theta}\)
D. \(\dfrac{4\sin\theta\cos\theta}{\sin^2\theta-\cos^2\theta}\)
E. \(\dfrac{2}{\sin^2\theta-\cos^2\theta}\)

\(AB\) and \(CD\) are two buildings of heights \(h\) and \(d\) respectively. The angles of elevation of \(C\) from \(A\) and \(B\) are respectively \(\theta\) and \(45^\circ\). \(d=\)
A. \(h(1-\tan\theta)\)
B. \(h(1+\tan\theta)\)
C. \(h\tan\theta\)
D. \(\dfrac{h}{1+\tan\theta}\)
E. \(\dfrac{h}{1-\tan\theta}\)

In the figure, \(BP\) is a diameter of the circle. The minor arc \(AB\) and the radius are of equal length. \(\angle APB=\)
A. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\text{ rad}\)
B. \(1\text{ rad}\)
C. \(\dfrac{\pi}{6}\text{ rad}\)
D. \(\dfrac{\pi}{4}\text{ rad}\)
E. \(\dfrac{\pi}{3}\text{ rad}\)

How many roots has the equation \(\sin\theta+\sin^2\theta=\cos^2\theta\) where \(0^\circ\le\theta\le360^\circ\)?
A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(2\)
D. \(3\)
E. \(4\)

If \(0\le x\le\pi\) and \(\sin x\le\cos x\), what is the range of \(x\)?
A. \(0\le x\le\dfrac{\pi}{4}\)
B. \(0\le x\le\dfrac{\pi}{2}\)
C. \(\dfrac{\pi}{4}\le x\le\dfrac{\pi}{2}\)
D. \(\dfrac{\pi}{4}\le x\le\pi\)
E. \(\dfrac{\pi}{2}\le x\le\pi\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a square of side \(2a\). \(M\) and \(N\) are the mid-points of \(AB\) and \(CD\) respectively. \(h\) is the height of the parallelogram \(MBND\). \(h=\)
A. \(\dfrac{1}{2}a\)
B. \(\dfrac{2}{\sqrt{5}}a\)
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{5}}{2}a\)
D. \(\dfrac{2}{\sqrt{3}}a\)
E. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{4}a\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangle. \(AC\) and \(BC\) intersect at \(K\). \(PAK\) is an equilateral triangle. \(\angle PBK=\)
A. \(48^\circ\)
B. \(50^\circ\)
C. \(52^\circ\)
D. \(54^\circ\)
E. \(60^\circ\)

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. \(PQ\) is a diameter. Which of the following is/are true?
I. \(a=b\)
II. \(c=2a\)
III. \(c+d=180^\circ\)
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

In the figure, the length of the minor arc \(CD\) is half the length of the minor arc \(BC\). \(\angle ACD=\)
A. \(30^\circ\)
B. \(35^\circ\)
C. \(40^\circ\)
D. \(45^\circ\)
E. \(50^\circ\)

In the figure, \(TP\) and \(TQ\) touch the circle at \(P\) and \(Q\) respectively. \(R\) is the point on \(TQ\) produced such that \(PR\) passes through the centre \(O\) of the circle. \(\angle QPR=\)
A. \(55^\circ\)
B. \(40^\circ\)
